subject: Accounting
title: Benford's Law in Fraud Detection
description: Learn Benford's Law in Fraud Detection in one minute.

hook.text: In real financial data, the digit 1 appears as the leading number about 30% of the time. Not 11%.

buildup.text: Benford's Law predicts the frequency of leading digits in naturally occurring number sets.

discovery.text: Fabricated numbers don't follow this pattern—fraudsters tend to distribute digits too evenly.

twist.text: Running a Benford analysis on expense reports can instantly flag datasets that look artificial.

climax.text: This mathematical law has been used in tax fraud cases, election audits, and corporate investigations.

punchline.text: Nature follows Benford's Law. Fraudsters rarely do.

quiz.question: What does Benford's Law predict?
options: The frequency of leading digits in natural number sets
The total revenue a company will earn
The probability of an employee committing fraud
